---
title: "Installation"
sidebar: getting-started
---

## Requirements

- **Python ≥ 3.11**
- No pandas dependency — yasqat uses polars throughout

## Install from PyPI

```bash
pip install yasqat
```

With [uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv) (recommended):

```bash
uv add yasqat
```

## Verify the installation

```python
import yasqat
print(yasqat.__version__)   # 0.3.1
```

## Optional: Databricks / cloud notebooks

yasqat works in Databricks, Google Colab, and any cloud notebook that supports Python 3.11+.
Install at cell level:

```python
%pip install yasqat
```

## Development install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/rexarski/yasqat.git
cd yasqat

# Create virtual environment and install with dev dependencies
uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"

# Run tests
uv run pytest

# Lint and type-check
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy src/yasqat/
```

## Dependencies

yasqat pulls in the following packages automatically:

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| [polars](https://pola.rs) | DataFrames — never pandas |
| [numpy](https://numpy.org) | Numeric arrays and distance matrices |
| [plotnine](https://plotnine.org) | ggplot2-grammar visualization |
| [numba](https://numba.pydata.org) | JIT-compiled distance inner loops |
| [scipy](https://scipy.org) | Hierarchical clustering linkage |
| [pyarrow](https://arrow.apache.org) | polars interop bridge |

::: {.callout-note}
yasqat targets Python 3.11, 3.12, and 3.13. It is tested on all three in CI.
:::
